title: heterogonium gurupahense
intro: Heterogonium gurupahense is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Heterogonium gurupahense include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential use as an anti-cancer agent. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve boiling the leaves in water or making a decoction, which can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to the affected area.
